display.purpose: Hero headline and section headings — Bricolage Grotesque, expressive and warm.

body.purpose: Prose and UI — Hanken Grotesk, neutral humanist, does the reading work.

mono.purpose: Literal terminal output, commands, code, package names — JetBrains Mono only.

shadows.name: shadow-card
shadows.purpose: Quiet lift on secondary buttons, the GitHub nav pill, and Expressive Code frames.

shadows.name: shadow-term
shadows.purpose: The hero terminal only — a warm pink-tinted glow lifting the signature object.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default easing for hovers and state transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Pink pill with dark ink text — the main CTA (View on GitHub).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem; font-family:var(--font-sans); font-weight:600; font-size:1.0625rem; text-decoration:none; padding:0.85rem 1.5rem; border-radius:var(--r-full); background:var(--pink); color:oklch(0.2 0.02 335); box-shadow:0 1px 0 oklch(1 0 0 / 0.35) inset, 0 8px 20px -10px oklch(0.6 0.19 352 / 0.7); transition:transform 130ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), background-color 130ms, box-shadow 220ms; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background:var(--pink-hover); transform:translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:2.5px solid var(--pink-link); outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Surface pill with a hairline that borders pink on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:0.5rem; font-family:var(--font-sans); font-weight:600; font-size:1.0625rem; text-decoration:none; padding:0.85rem 1.5rem; border-radius:var(--r-full); background:var(--surface); color:var(--ink); border:1px solid var(--border-strong); box-shadow:0 1px 2px oklch(0.3 0.03 320 / 0.05), 0 6px 18px -12px oklch(0.3 0.05 320 / 0.14); transition:border-color 130ms, transform 130ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color:var(--pink); transform:translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline:2.5px solid var(--pink-link); outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Pink-soft pill with a leading icon; kicker for the hero and sections.
components.html: Gleam supply-chain auditing
components.css: .ds-badge { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:0.45rem; font-family:var(--font-sans); font-size:0.9375rem; font-weight:600; color:var(--pink-link); background:var(--pink-soft); border:1px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--pink) 25%, transparent); padding:0.35rem 0.75rem 0.35rem 0.6rem; border-radius:var(--r-full); }

components.name: GitHub Nav Pill
components.description: Bordered surface pill in the sticky nav; lifts on hover.
components.css: .ds-navgh { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:0.45rem; font-family:var(--font-sans); font-weight:600; font-size:0.9375rem; color:var(--ink); text-decoration:none; padding:0.5rem 0.85rem; border:1px solid var(--border-strong); border-radius:var(--r-full); background:var(--surface); transition:border-color 130ms, transform 130ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), box-shadow 130ms; } .ds-navgh:hover { border-color:var(--pink); transform:translateY(-1px); box-shadow:0 1px 2px oklch(0.3 0.03 320 / 0.05), 0 6px 18px -12px oklch(0.3 0.05 320 / 0.14); }

components.name: Active Docs Link
components.description: Sidebar item in its active state — a pink-soft pill, no side-stripe.
components.css: .ds-docslink { display:block; padding:0.4rem 0.7rem; border-radius:var(--r-sm); color:var(--ink-2); text-decoration:none; font-family:var(--font-sans); font-size:0.9375rem; font-weight:500; } .ds-docslink .ds-mono { font-family:var(--font-mono); font-size:0.92em; } .ds-docslink:hover { background:var(--bg-sunken); color:var(--ink); } .ds-docslink.is-active { background:var(--pink-soft); color:var(--pink-link); font-weight:600; }

components.name: Terminal Window
components.description: The signature dark terminal — window chrome + a mono body.
components.html:
licence_audit — check
$ licence_audit check
components.css: .ds-term { background:var(--term-bg); border:1px solid var(--term-border); border-radius:var(--r-lg); overflow:hidden; box-shadow:0 2px 6px oklch(0.25 0.04 320 / 0.16), 0 34px 64px -26px oklch(0.55 0.16 350 / 0.42); } .ds-term-bar { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; padding:0.7rem 1rem; background:var(--term-bar); border-bottom:1px solid var(--term-border); } .ds-dot { width:0.72rem; height:0.72rem; border-radius:var(--r-full); background:color-mix(in oklch, var(--term-fg) 22%, transparent); } .ds-dot-pink { background:var(--pink); } .ds-term-title { font-family:var(--font-mono); font-size:0.78rem; color:var(--term-dim); } .ds-term-body { margin:0; padding:1.1rem 1.4rem; font-family:var(--font-mono); font-size:0.9rem; line-height:1.75; color:var(--term-fg); } .ds-sig { color:var(--pink-bright); font-weight:700; }

narrative.overview: licence_audit tells you the truth about your dependencies, warmly — and the website is that same terminal rendered as a page. A calm off-white ground, a single dark terminal as the hero, a warm pink that nods to Gleam without impersonating it, and a status palette lifted straight from the CLI's own report glyphs (green = allowed, red = denied, grey = skipped/unknown). The design demonstrates the product instead of describing it. The voice is playful and approachable but never sloppy; confidence comes from being clear and correct, in the spirit of Charm's warmth and Bun/Biome's code-forward directness.
keyCharacteristics: Warm pink (oklch(0.66 0.19 352)) as a deliberate Gleam nod, used as accent — never drenching.
A semantic status palette (pass green / deny red / muted grey) drawn from the CLI's glyphs.
Real terminal output as the hero, not abstract feature copy.
One dark terminal surface on an otherwise flat, off-white page.
Playful, approachable display type over an exacting, high-contrast reading experience.

rules.name: The Report-Truth Rule
rules.body: Status colours mean exactly what they mean in the CLI. Never use deny-red decoratively or pass-green for a non-passing thing; the palette is documentation, so keep it honest.

rules.name: The Pink-Nod Rule
rules.body: Pink signals kinship with Gleam, never identity theft. It carries accents, one CTA field, and the mark — never a drenched surface.

rules.name: The One-Terminal Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one family of dark surface: the terminal (hero, Expressive Code blocks). Everything else is flat and light.

rules.name: The Mono-Is-Real Rule
rules.body: Monospace is reserved for things that are literally code or terminal output. If it's brand copy, it's the sans.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Bricolage speaks (display, personality); Hanken reads (body, UI). They never swap roles.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. The hero terminal is the single sanctioned exception; depth elsewhere is tone and spacing, not shadow.

dos: Do put real audit output on the page — the actual ✓ ✗ ? · glyphs, tree indentation, and status colours — as the hero. Show, don't tell.
Do keep status colours semantically honest: green = allowed, red = denied, grey = skipped/unknown.
Do reserve monospace for literal terminal output, commands, and code.
Do lead with the install command and the GitHub CTA.
Do hold body text to 65–75ch and verify every text colour clears WCAG AA.
Do keep motion to one signature hero moment plus quiet feedback; ship an already-visible default and give every animation a reduced-motion path.
Do add min-width: 0 to any grid item holding a code block so it scrolls internally instead of blowing out the track.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS landing page: no gradient hero, no three identical feature cards, no big-number stat band, no purple-blue gradients.
Don't reach for the corporate enterprise-security look: no navy-and-gold compliance theatre, no stock photos of locks or shields, no fear-based selling.
Don't let pink drench the surface or impersonate Gleam's identity — it's an accent and a nod.
Don't use a warm-tinted cream/sand background; the ground is a calm, true off-white (oklch(0.985 0.004 340)).
Don't use monospace as shorthand for 'technical.' If it isn't real code or output, it's the sans.
Don't use border-left/right > 1px as a coloured accent stripe; use a pink-soft fill, a full border, or a leading glyph.
Don't gate content visibility on a class-triggered reveal; the report must render even when the animation never fires.
